sections.paragraph:
δραπέτης (δρᾱπέτης, ου,) n (dra_pet hs_ou stem_acc masc)

n (drhpet hs_ou stem_acc masc ionic)

n (dra_petid hs_ou stem_acc masc Mosch.)

n (dra_pet is_idos stem_acc Anth.)

n (dra_petisk os_ou stem_acc masc diminutive Luc.)


Etym. διδράσκω

1. a runaway, Lat. fugitivus, βασιλέος from the king, Hdt.:— a runaway slave, Hdt.

2. as adj., runaway, fugitive, δραπέτης κλῆρος a lot of fugitive kind, i. e. crumbling clod of earth, which could not be drawn out of the urn, Soph.
